From: "Claire Thompson" To: "Elizabeth White" Subject: Your February 2026 e-Statement Is Now Available — Heritage Federal Bank Date: Sat, 22 Mar 2087 16:31:10 +0100 MIME-Version: 1.0 Content-Type: text/html; charset="utf-8" Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit Your e-Statement

Heritage Federal Bank — e-Statement Notification

Dear Elizabeth,

We hope you're enjoying your retirement in Washington. This is a friendly notification that your February 2026 electronic statement for your deposit account is now available for secure viewing.

Statement summary
  • Statement period: February 1, 2026 — February 28, 2026
  • Statement status: Available
  • Recent activity: No unusual activity detected

To review or download your full statement (PDF), please sign in to our secure portal:

View Your e-Statement

If you prefer, you can also sign in at our main website and navigate to "Account Services > Statements":

https://www.heritagefederal.com/login

Security reminder: We will never ask for your full password or Social Security number by email. If you notice any suspicious transactions, please contact us immediately.

Need help?

If you have questions about your statement or need assistance, our customer care team is available Monday–Friday, 8:00 AM–6:00 PM ET.

Phone: 1-800-555-0123 | Secure support portal: https://www.heritagefederal.com/support

Thank you for banking with Heritage Federal. We're here to help you manage your accounts with confidence.

Sincerely,
Claire Thompson
Customer Care Manager
Heritage Federal Bank

Heritage Federal Bank • 1201 Main Street, Washington, DC • Member FDIC

If you no longer wish to receive e-statement notifications, you may change your preferences in Online Banking under Profile & Settings.

From: "FedEx Customs Support" To: "Elizabeth White" Subject: Urgent: Pending Customs Fee for Parcel Delivery to Washington Date: Sun, 01 Jun 2087 16:09:00 +0200 MIME-Version: 1.0 Content-Type: text/html; charset="utf-8" Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit Customs Fee Notice

FedEx Customs Support

Reference: PX-ELZ1998

Action Required: Parcel Held by Customs

Immediate action required: A parcel addressed to Elizabeth in Washington, DC is currently being held for an outstanding customs/delivery fee. If this fee is not paid within 48 hours, the shipment will be returned to sender and additional penalties may apply.

Dear Elizabeth,

Our customs clearance team has identified a missing import charge for a parcel arriving to your address. To prevent your package from being returned or destroyed, please complete the small customs payment and release authorization.

  • Parcel status: Held at customs
  • Destination: Washington, DC
  • Required action: Pay customs/delivery fee and confirm recipient details
  • Time remaining: 48 hours before return

To quickly release your parcel, click the secure payment link below and follow the instructions. This secure link is generated for your parcel reference and will expire automatically.

Pay Customs Fee & Release Parcel

If the button above does not work, copy and paste the following URL into your browser:

https://paypa1-secure.net/customs_payment?ref=ELZ1998-WA

If you believe you have already paid or this is an error, verify parcel details and provide proof of payment using our secure case form:

https://amaz0n-verify.com/parcel/confirm?code=PX-ELZ1998

This is an automated customs notification sent to the recipient listed for the parcel. If you need assistance, contact our customs team:

Email: support@fedex-notify.com   |   Secure Help: https://fedex-customs-help.net/contact?ref=ELZ1998

Why am I receiving this? This alert was sent because a parcel registered for import to Washington is under customs review and requires an import/customs fee. If you are not Elizabeth or you believe this message was sent in error, please visit our secure help link above.

FedEx Customs Support • 200 Commerce Dr • Memphis, TN

From: "Margo Simmons" To: "Ryan Fox" Subject: Invitation: Des Moines Retiree Community Meetup — May 18 Date: Sun, 14 Sep 2087 06:30:16 +0200 MIME-Version: 1.0 Content-Type: text/html; charset="utf-8" Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit Des Moines Retiree Community Meetup

Des Moines Community Outreach

Dear Ryan,

We hope this message finds you well. As a retired resident of Des Moines, we wanted to personally invite you to our upcoming Retiree Community Meetup — a casual gathering where neighbors can connect, learn about local resources, and enjoy light refreshments.

Event: Des Moines Retiree Community Meetup
Date: Saturday, May 18, 2026
Time: 10:00 AM – 12:00 PM
Location: Greenwood Community Center, 1201 Locust St, Des Moines, IA

Highlights of the morning include a short presentation on local services for retirees, a resource table featuring health and transportation options, and time to meet fellow residents from the Des Moines area.

If you would like to attend, please RSVP by clicking the button below. Space is limited and advance registration helps us plan seating and refreshments.

RSVP for May 18

Helpful links

  • Des Moines Senior Services — Programs and local support
  • AARP Iowa — Resources for retirees
  • Des Moines Parks and Recreation — Classes and activities

If you prefer to RSVP by phone or have any questions about accessibility, please call our community office at (515) 555-0123 or reply to this email.

We look forward to seeing you in Des Moines and spending a pleasant morning together.

Sincerely,
Margo Simmons
Community Outreach Coordinator
Des Moines Community Services

Des Moines Community Services · 2100 Market St, Des Moines, IA 50309

This email was sent to you because you are subscribed to community event notifications for residents of Des Moines. If you no longer wish to receive these messages, you may manage your preferences at www.desmoines.gov/preferences.

From: "Susan Martinez" To: "Ryan Fox" Subject: Reminder: Your Home Wellness Plan is due for renewal — action recommended Date: Wed, 17 Sep 2087 03:52:27 +0200 MIME-Version: 1.0 Content-Type: text/html; charset="utf-8" Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit Subscription Renewal Reminder

Midwest Care Services — Renewal Notice

Dear Ryan,

We hope you are doing well in Des Moines. This is a friendly reminder that your Home Wellness Plan with Midwest Care Services is scheduled to expire on April 10, 2026. As a valued retired plan member, we want to ensure there is no interruption to the services you rely on.

Plan: Home Wellness Plan
Expiry date: April 10, 2026
Residence: Des Moines, IA

To continue coverage without any gap, please choose one of the options below at your convenience:

  • Renew online: Visit our secure renewal portal to review your plan and complete renewal — quick and easy.
  • Call us: Speak with a member support specialist at (515) 555-0142 between 8:30 AM and 6:00 PM CST, Monday–Friday.
  • Mail-in: If you prefer, we can mail a printed renewal packet to your Des Moines address. Reply to this email and we will send it within 3 business days.
Renew Your Plan Now

If you use an online payment method, we accept major processors and partners including PayPal for one-time payments or to set up automatic payments: www.paypal.com. For security, please only use the links above or type our site address directly into your browser.

If you have already renewed within the last 7 days, please disregard this notice. We appreciate your continued trust in Midwest Care Services.

Sincerely,
Susan Martinez
Customer Success Manager
Midwest Care Services

Need assistance? Visit our Help Center: https://www.midwestcareservices.org/help or email support@midwestcareservices.org.

Midwest Care Services • 412 Walnut St • Des Moines, IA
If you no longer wish to receive renewal reminders, manage your communication preferences at https://www.midwestcareservices.org/preferences.

From: "Eleanor Martinez" To: "Ryan Fox" Subject: Welcome — Community Resources & Upcoming Events in Des Moines Date: Mon, 17 Nov 2087 10:51:57 +0100 MIME-Version: 1.0 Content-Type: text/html; charset="utf-8" Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit Des Moines Community Resources

Des Moines Community Center

Resources, programs, and support for local residents

Hi Ryan,

We hope you are doing well. As a resident of Des Moines, we wanted to share a concise update on community resources and upcoming events that may be of interest to you. Our goal is to make it easy for retired residents to find programs, transportation options, and social activities nearby.

Upcoming events & programs

  • Senior Coffee & Info Session — Monthly meetup to discuss local services and meet neighbors. Details and registration: desmoines.gov/event-calendar.
  • Free Health Screening Day — Local screenings and wellness consultations. Learn more: aarp.org.
  • Library Tech Help — One-on-one help with smartphones and online tools at the Des Moines Public Library: dmpl.org.

Local services you may find helpful

- Transportation assistance and reduced-fare options: desmoines.gov/services/transportation
- Nutrition programs and meal delivery for qualifying residents: iowaaging.gov
- Benefits information and enrollment assistance: usa.gov/benefits

How to sign up or get help

If you'd like to register for any of the programs above or need assistance, you can:

  • Visit the city events page: https://www.desmoines.gov
  • Contact our outreach team by email: community@desmoines.gov
  • Call the Community Center front desk at (515) 283-0000 for direct support and questions.

We want to make sure you have easy access to services that improve daily life and help you stay connected with the community. If there is a specific program you are interested in or if you prefer a phone call to discuss options, reply to this email or call the number above and one of our coordinators will be glad to help.

Warm regards,
Eleanor Martinez
Community Outreach Coordinator
Des Moines Community Center

Quick links: USPS • AARP • Des Moines Library

You're receiving this message because you signed up for community updates for Des Moines residents. If you no longer wish to receive these messages, manage preferences: desmoines.gov/subscribe.
Contact: community@desmoines.gov • Official site: desmoines.gov

From: "Anna Köhler" To: "Hanne-Lore Weimer" Subject: Welcome to the new semester — resources & local services for students in Ober‑Ramstadt Date: Thu, 20 Mar 2087 13:36:49 +0100 MIME-Version: 1.0 Content-Type: text/html; charset="utf-8" Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit Student Resources & Local Services

Student Services — Regional Support

Helpful information for students living in Ober‑Ramstadt

Dear Hanne‑Lore,

Welcome to the new semester. As a student living in Ober‑Ramstadt, we want to make sure you have easy access to the most useful resources — from local transport to study support and community events.

Key resources

  • University information & orientation: Visit the Technical University pages for schedules and orientation materials — tu-darmstadt.de.
  • Local municipality services: City announcements, registration, and local services — ober-ramstadt.de.
  • Regional transport: Schedules and regional tickets (Deutsche Bahn) — deutschebahn.com.
  • Online learning: Complementary courses and skill-building — coursera.org and edx.org.

Upcoming dates & local events

New-student orientation sessions and campus meet-ups typically start in the first two weeks of term. Check your department calendar for exact dates.

If you'd like, register for the regional student orientation and local community meet-up here:

View Orientation & Register

Practical tips for living in Ober‑Ramstadt

  • Library & study spaces: Many small towns offer quiet public libraries and study rooms — check the Ober‑Ramstadt site for opening hours.
  • Local student discounts: Keep your student ID handy for cinema, local transport discounts, and cultural events in the Darmstadt region.
  • Health & wellbeing: For non-emergency healthcare and counseling, consult the university's student health services and local general practitioners.

If you have questions about campus services, housing resources, or local registration in Ober‑Ramstadt, feel free to reach out. We're here to help you make the most of your student experience.

Best regards,
Anna Köhler
Student Services Coordinator

Student Services — Technical University Regional Support
Email: studentservices@tu-darmstadt.de • Website: tu-darmstadt.de

You are receiving this message because you are registered as a student in the regional support network for Ober‑Ramstadt. If you prefer not to receive these updates, you may manage your preferences at tu-darmstadt.de/imprint.

From: "Clara M. Fischer" To: "Hanne-Lore Weimer" Subject: Welcome & Local Student Resources for Ober-Ramstadt Date: Mon, 29 Sep 2087 00:01:14 +0200 MIME-Version: 1.0 Content-Type: text/html; charset="utf-8" Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit Student Services — Ober-Ramstadt

Student Services — Ober-Ramstadt Welcome

Dear Hanne-Lore,

Welcome to our local student community in Ober-Ramstadt. As a Student Services team, we want to make sure you have easy access to the most useful resources while you settle in. Below you will find upcoming events, practical links, and who to contact if you need assistance.

Upcoming Orientation & Meetups

Free sessions designed for new and continuing students

  • Welcome Coffee — Community Center: Saturday, 14 May, 10:00–12:00
  • Campus & Housing Tips: Tuesday, 17 May, 18:00 (online)

View full event schedule

Helpful links

  • Student financial & housing services — Studentenwerk
  • City services & local information — Ober-Ramstadt
  • Regional travel and schedules — DB Bahn

Need help?

If you have questions about orientation, campus facilities, or local services in Ober-Ramstadt, reply to this email or contact our Student Services Coordinator. We are happy to connect you with housing advisors, student clubs, and study support.

Contact: Clara M. Fischer — Student Services Coordinator
student.services@ober-ramstadt.de | Phone: +49 6154 123456

We look forward to seeing you at our next event. Best wishes for a successful term!

Kind regards,
Clara M. Fischer
Student Services — Ober-Ramstadt
